metadata.title: Research the EGFR Resistance Playbook with Claude: T790M, C797S, and MET Amplification
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Nik Bear Brown. Research it with Claude. Then take it apart.

An EGFR inhibitor works beautifully for eleven months. Then the cancer grows back. The resistance mutation was there from the start — too rare to detect — waiting while the drug killed every cell that could compete with it. The treatment created the conditions for its own failure.
Ask Claude to map the resistance mechanisms.

props.command: claude "Research acquired resistance to osimertinib in EGFR-mutant non-small-cell lung cancer. For each of the three main mechanisms — C797S mutation, MET amplification, and small-cell transformation — explain the molecular basis, how it is detected (ctDNA vs. tissue biopsy vs. FISH), what next-line treatments exist, and the evidence level. Build a clinical decision tree for managing acquired resistance."

beats.narration_text: Read the brief before trusting it.

props.code:
# egfr_resistance_brief.md  --  Claude output

## C797S Mutation
- Mechanism: allosteric resistance; in cis with T790M
- Detection: ctDNA deep sequencing
- Treatment: EAI045 (investigational); no approved std of care

## MET Amplification (~15-20% of cases)
- Mechanism: bypass signaling around EGFR
- Detection: FISH or ctDNA
- Treatment: osimertinib + savolitinib (TATTON trial data)

## SCLC Transformation (~5%)
- Mechanism: phenotypic transformation; Rb loss
- Detection: tissue biopsy required
- Treatment: EP/EC chemotherapy (SCLC protocol)

beats.narration_text: The decision tree animates: osimertinib resistance splits three ways — C797S allosteric mutation detected by ctDNA deep sequencing, no approved standard of care; MET amplification detected by FISH or ctDNA, treatable with osimertinib plus savolitinib; small-cell transformation requiring tissue biopsy and SCLC chemotherapy protocol. Three mechanisms, three detection paths, three treatment approaches.

props.command: claude "Could pre-treatment ctDNA deep sequencing — looking for subclonal T790M or MET — identify which patients will develop which resistance mechanism before resistance occurs?"

beats.narration_text: Pre-resistance monitoring analysis: ctDNA ultra-deep sequencing at baseline can detect subclonal T790M at variant allele fractions of 0.1-0.3% — below standard clinical detection threshold but present in 15-25% of treatment-naive EGFR-mutant NSCLC. AURA trial retrospective analyses show pre-existing subclonal T790M predicts faster osimertinib resistance. Pre-existing subclonal MET amplification detectable in approximately 8% at baseline by ultra-deep ctDNA. Implication: baseline ultra-deep ctDNA may allow planning the resistance sequence before resistance occurs — but no prospective trial has validated this as a treatment decision tool.

The treatment was a selection event. Osimertinib did exactly what it was designed to do — killed T790M-bearing cells — and in doing so, eliminated every competitor the C797S or MET-amplified subclone had. The resistance pathways are not random; they are mechanistically predictable from the EGFR signaling network. The decision tree maps detection to therapy; the sequencing-resistance approach maps the selection event before it happens.
Next: research how chronic inflammation — a wound that never heals — creates the molecular conditions for carcinogenesis.
Nik Bear Brown. Research it with Claude. At nikbearbrown.com.
